Hello. My name is Saty. I am a happily married dad of four. I have been trading equities since 1997. But before I went full-time in 2020, I spent 20 years as a tech founder and executive. In 2020, I decided to retire. That retirement turned into a passion for full-time trading. In 2021, looking for a way to trade both directions with fixed risk, I began to learn how to trade options.

Witnessing the sheer amount of nonsensical financial “wisdom” in social and traditional media over the years, I thought maybe I could bring some simplicity and sanity to the world. I am now on a personal mission to help simplify trading through innovative products and timeless concepts. I share my learnings and work in an effort to improve the lives of millions of others. I hope I can be helpful to you. 🙏

You can get all my indicators for free from my website: https://t.co/rXzyzG2lwv

If you want to learn more about how I trade, how to use my indicators, or watch my weekly market analysis you can subscribe for free to my YouTube Channel: https://t.co/pM5ztvX745

And finally, back in 2022, a lot of folks asked to have more of my time, so I created an education-focused discord. If you want to learn from me and trade with me, you can join me and my wonderful community here: https://t.co/hZZaqiEMDr

For those who have been here for a while, thanks for your support. Drop me a note below. It's always nice to hear from you.

Scammers
Be careful! I will never reach out in a comment or DM and solicit you for anything. My username is satymahajan on Twitter, YouTube, Discord. I don't have anything else.

Good morning. Clean downside move overnight on tech sell. Back at weekly lows. Be mindful we're at the bottom of the 10m range.
- If we get continuation in momentum below this key weekly support, -1 ATR is just below. Otherwise, pullback and resistance again in 10m trend, can revisit this weekly support and then -1 ATR
- If we start to mean revert here, Gap Fill Thursday is in play back toward the 10m 21 and short trigger. But be mindful, the mean reversion / GFT can be cut short if the downtrend continues with resistance at the ribbon.

Hi everyone, after 6 years of anonymity, here I am. Brando in the flesh.

I’m a day trader with 14 years of experience. 

I started with $6000 and after many ups and downs I ended up living the life of my dreams.

Here’s my story below. 👇 

I grew up on the east coast near Boston. My Mom and Dad were war refugees from Vietnam. They came to the United States on a small boat 35 years ago in search of a better life. They did their best to raise me and offer the best life they could when I was growing up. I’ve lived a very normal and quiet life until I was 19 years old. This is when I discovered the stock market. One day,I looked at the company American Apparel and the stock price  in the library and saw the potential of how much money could be made if I bought at a certain price and sold at a higher one. A friend of mine introduced me to options shortly after and the rest was history. I graduated from College and continued my journey towards becoming a profitable trader.

The first 3 years are BY FAR the hardest. The number of strategies, indicators, stocks, and education available was a sensory overload. I didn't know what to use or what to read. So I just read them all. I was fortunate in my first few years to get lucky trading a few stocks such as $BAC $INO, and some weed stocks during that 2013 range that helped build up my account tremendously. 

After the first three years I realized that LESS is MORE. You don’t need 10 indicators to tell you a stock is going to go up. You don’t need 5 different strategies to be a profitable trader. You just need ONE. The most important thing that I found was helpful is PATIENCE. 99% of the world is impatient. I knew If I could master this, I could have a big edge against the majority of retail traders.

I spent a lot of time meditating. I spent hundreds of hours sitting alone in silence trying to clear my mind and focus on the present. Eventually I got really good at it. I noticed my ability to wait for trades got better. In my 4th and 5th year of trading, I was able to wait days to weeks at a time for the best trades. This was one of the A-HA moments I had in trading early on. Since then I’ve refined and mastered the art of patience and leveraged it to my advantage.

Fast forward 14 years. I’ve retired my parents. I’ve helped my brother, and countless others. I ask for nothing in return from anyone. I don’t ask for any favors and I certainly do not try and steal from anyone. I’ve lived my life with the utmost integrity and I did things MY WAY. I can’t be bought and sold. Money and Power have zero influence in my decision making.  My sole purpose on this earth is to help as many people as possible.

I look forward to teaching you all and guiding everyone through the stock market for many years to come. Cheers! 🙏

Elite traders aren't focused on the money.

They're focused on getting their process so dialed in they could trade in their sleep.

I've been in the game for 16 years.

The months playing bounty hunter were my worst months.

But shifting my focus to execution turned $10,000 into $100,000 into $1,000,000 and so on…

I didn’t focus on the results.

I just wanted to get better. And by wanting to get better I made more money.

The best traders in the world agree on one thing:

You need to want to grind to get good.

Read that last line again.

TRADE PLAN for LOTTO Friday 📈 

$SPX closed -90 on the day. If SPX gives up 7400 and closes under tomorrow we can see another dip towards 7330-7340 leading into FOMC on Wednesday. I would treat the upside as a quicker day trade until we see 7500 again.

$QQQ almost dropped back to the June lows near 686 again but held and closed near 692. As long as 686 holds the uptrend is still intact this summer. QQQ under 686 can drop another 20-25 pts.

$MU will test 1250 again this year. We need to see if MU can continue to form a base above 900 into August.
MU July 24 1020C is best as a lotto above 1000

Good luck tmrw everyone!! 🫡

You rarely hear anyone say "[insert 9-5] changed my life".

You often hear people say "trading changed my life".

But somehow trading is a scam and a 9-5 isn’t.

Funny how that works.

Losing streaks are part of trading.

Every trader I respect has had one.

I've had them too.

The market doesn't care about our streak.

It doesn't care about our feelings.

It’s just waves in the ocean.

Learn what you can from every loss and move on.

This is the best framework for trading:

- Weekly chart for major levels
- Daily chart to mark them up
- Wait for price to arrive
- Enter when it's there
- Walk away

Keep a simple and repeatable trading routine.

Every morning before the market opens, I check futures first. 

What happened overnight? Trending or consolidating?

Then I:

- check economic events like FOMC, CPI, earnings
- mark 3-5 clean levels on the daily and weekly
- build a watchlist of 3-4 names

Takes about 30 minutes.

Been doing this every morning at 4:30am for 16 years.

By the time market opens I know exactly what I'm waiting for.

Your trades are only as good as your habits.

Pros don’t predict where price might go.

Pros react to what it's doing.

Stop telling fortunes.

Start trading.
A winning strategy won’t save your losing psychology.

$MU lost $350B in market cap in a week, then made half of it back in two days

$MU is right outside of 1000, up almost 200 pts after a violent backtest of 800. Through 1000 and $MU can quickly retest ATHs, through ATHs and it'll gravitate towards 1500. 845 and 800 absolutely need to hold, otherwise $MU can see a flush towards 650

 Key Catalysts
Memory price shock: Morgan Stanley now sees Q3 memory prices rising 25% from Q2 on AI-driven shortages, the single biggest driver of the recent rally

HBM sold out through 2027: $MU has locked in production volumes two years out, shifting power from competing on share to negotiating on price with hyperscalers.

Buyback math: UBS projects up to $400B in free cash flow through 2028, enough to retire roughly 40% of shares outstanding if management chooses to deploy it that way

1000 is the key inflection point for the move back to ATHs and towards $1.5T MCAP at 1327. Through 1327 and  buyers will quickly push $MU towards 1500.

 Trade Ideas
$MU over 1000
Day Trade: 7/24 1000C
Swing Trade: 8/21 1300C

Last week's selloff was a sentiment problem, not a demand problem. The chips are still sold out.

Best trading psychology ranking in 2026:

S-tier:

- Detachment
- Process over outcome
- Patience is the strategy

A-tier:

- Cash is a position
- Every loss teaches a lesson
- Consistency beats perfection

D-tier:

- I just need one big trade
- This setup feels different
- I'll make it back tomorrow

In 10 years you will be making your yearly salary in about 7 minutes.

You will be so proud of yourself for marching through the fog.

Don’t let yourself down. Keep trying.

Go from hobby trader to professional trader in 4 steps:

1. Journal every trade
2. Walk away after 2 bad trades
3. Wake up before the market every day
4. Review your worst trades more than your best ones
BUY!!!!
